title: Speculation Return from the LCL sea Rule of tang
display_title: Speculation Return from the LCL sea Rule of tang
wikitext:
[]: Speculation: Return from the LCL-sea/Rule of tang             Quote Post by SEELE » Sun Apr 20, 2014 4:09 pm Hey guys, (i didnt found some relateable thread, sorry) something bothers me since yesterday. I ask myself: If Rei/Lilith is honest about the "returnees" then who might come back? We saw Ghost!Rei and Ghost!Kaworu who are resemble Adam and Lilith. But what bothers me is his apperance in EoE. He was dead. But why he remembers Shinji at all? Rei III doesnt remember her previous heroic sacrifice. Only Kaworu's "alter body" is merged with Lilith (even so Kawuro seems to be dead for a week from EoTV to EoE). So what bothers me is: Is it even possible for Kaji for example to come back? And for Rei II? Misato? Maya? Fuyutski? Asuka for example is torn apart (did Kaji save her? The JSSDF wouldn't save Asuka, and at NERV was pretty much everyone dead). (the rule seems to: But unless you can get tanged you could come back). Its just like E20 in NGE. As long as you identify yourself you are able to come back. Count this for Gendo too? He was bite in half. Apparently as long as Lilith gathered the soul through the tang ification (even with a dead body?) you come back? In case of Kaji i could think about the way he return. He imageend himself with Misato together. He doesnt wanted to die but he knows there is only one way to save Misato. Animals tend to have the urge feeling of survival. So did this count, too? Or is everything human-related "tanged"? What did you guys think? Shinji is the first returnee ... there is nothing to argue about.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Apr 21, 2014 9:11 am Er, yea, the state of death doesn't really matter. Everyone on the planet was turned into a disembodied soul, and all life on the planet is made out of LCL, so if people are able to re-establish their egos, they can make new bodies out of LCL automatically as if they returned to life. Ir's generally assumed that all plants and animals at the time did so automatically.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Apr 21, 2014 9:38 am AuraTwilight wrote: Er, yea, the state of death doesn't really matter. Everyone on the planet was turned into a disembodied soul, and all life on the planet is made out of LCL, so if people are able to re-establish their egos, they can make new bodies out of LCL automatically as if they returned to life. Ir's generally assumed that all plants and animals at the time did so automatically.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Mon Apr 21, 2014 4:05 pm Bagheera wrote: No idea how far back it goes.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Mon Apr 21, 2014 4:15 pm 1. I don't think plants have souls in-universe, and possibly not animals either. It makes more sense for Lilith's terraforming process to include the evolution of terrestrial life into a sapient, humanoid species that's suitable to receive those souls. Souls are clearly linked to sentience/sapience and carry the personality of the person in some way. Grass doesn't have a personality. 2. We have clear on screen evidence that souls are collected by Lilith after a natural death, and prior to the actual start of Third Impact. There is no reason to assume that Lilith didn't reach all the way back to the first humans to collect dead souls from everyone ever. Every human who ever lived is a lot of humans but that doesn't mean they're all coming back because (a) they may in fact be recycled/reincarnated and (b) people who lived long natural lives and died would most likely prefer to remain dead/be reincarnated rather than popping out of the sea in their old selves.

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Mon Apr 21, 2014 4:18 pm Considering that the goal of Instrumentality was a single, unified human soul, this theory makes sense.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Mon Apr 21, 2014 4:24 pm I don't know if the supplemental materials address this (and frankly I don't really care) but the whole Seed/Moon thing seems like an ideal way to overcome the issue of faster-than-light travel in a relativistic universe. You suck out everyone's soul, put it in a bio-magi-tech statis machine, and hurl it out into the void to eventually land and seed a planet with a whole ecosystem that will eventually support the re-ensouled beings. Obviously you include a set of instructions to repeat the process if it's needed again. Obviously it would be a bad idea to put all your souls in one egg so you send out multiples and hope they don't hit the same planet, which frankly is like trying to hit an individual atom with a bullet and shouldn't really happen anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Apr 21, 2014 4:35 pm If reincarnation IS in effect, (not necessarily sayin it is) those who've been dead long enough to reincarnate should not pop out. So probably no Naoko.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Apr 21, 2014 4:39 pm Kendrix wrote: If reincarnation IS in effect, (not necessarily sayin it is) those who've been dead long enough to reincarnate should not pop out. So probably no Naoko.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE » Mon Apr 21, 2014 4:47 pm Bagheera wrote: Yes, absolutely. If reincarnation's in play (and we have no reason to assume it's not) that means the living and the recently dead are fair game. Everyone else is off the table.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Apr 21, 2014 4:51 pm Not sure about Kaji, he's been dead for weeks at that point. He seems to have entered instrumentality in EoTv, but we don't see much of him in EoE's mindtrips. As for Yui, well, she chose to drift into space explicitly mentioning that she's doing so alone. Without her present and instrumentality undone, Gendo doesn't really have a reason to stay tanged, he'll probably take a while to go back, but one can assume that Yui had a nice long talk with him while they were both inside the GNR.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE » Mon Apr 21, 2014 5:15 pm Kendrix wrote: Not sure about Kaji, he's been dead for weeks at that point. He seems to have entered instrumentality in EoTv, but we don't see much of him in EoE's mindtrips. As for Yui, well, she chose to drift into space explicitly mentioning that she's doing so alone. Without her present and instrumentality undone, Gendo doesn't really have a reason to stay tanged, he'll probably take a while to go back, but one can assume that Yui had a nice long talk with him while they were both inside the GNR.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Apr 21, 2014 5:19 pm I remember one fic that had Keel reform, but without those implants he was dependant on, with administered him with a swift karmic death. I doubt they could pull of the whole plot again with Lillith & Adam molten to sludge and all EVAs destroyed or in space.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Mon Apr 21, 2014 5:20 pm Chuckman wrote: I don't know if the supplemental materials address this (and frankly I don't really care) but the whole Seed/Moon thing seems like an ideal way to overcome the issue of faster-than-light travel in a relativistic universe. You suck out everyone's soul, put it in a bio-magi-tech statis machine, and hurl it out into the void to eventually land and seed a planet with a whole ecosystem that will eventually support the re-ensouled beings. Obviously you include a set of instructions to repeat the process if it's needed again. Obviously it would be a bad idea to put all your souls in one egg so you send out multiples and hope they don't hit the same planet, which frankly is like trying to hit an individual atom with a bullet and shouldn't really happen anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Apr 21, 2014 5:22 pm Makes you wonder i the SoLs also have something like an emergency flight function à la Omega from FFVII, in case a planet goes bad.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Mon Apr 21, 2014 5:23 pm ElMariachi wrote: The only big unknown is how the hell the Black Moon ended on Earth too, but that's something that'll never be answered.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Apr 21, 2014 5:25 pm In my headcanon, the black moon was originally meant for Mars or Venus. The latter at least is the same size as the earth and capable of holding an atmosphere, now if we only had that nifty terraforming technology to make said atmosphere less lethal...

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Mon Apr 21, 2014 5:26 pm ElMariachi wrote: According to the NGE2 videogame and the CI, that's exactly the deal with the Moons. Instead of searching for FTL travel means that they probably didn't found (if its even possible), they worked around it by split their souls, putting them into the Moons and launched them in space toward suitable worlds and wait whatever long it'll take (according to the CI, it took a couple billions years for the White and Black Moon to reach Earth, and at least one other White Moon is still in space!) The only big unknown is how the hell the Black Moon ended on Earth too, but that's something that'll never be answered.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE » Mon Apr 21, 2014 5:28 pm Kendrix wrote: I remember one fic that had Keel reform, but without those implants he was dependant on, with administered him with a swift karmic death. I doubt they could pull of the whole plot again with Lillith & Adam molten to sludge and all EVAs destroyed or in space.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Apr 21, 2014 5:28 pm The angels would probably be just fine on Venus, tho. If only we could shoot them over there, we might have peacefully shared the system....
